programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Greg Davies is the 'Taskmaster' in this game show that challenges the wit and wisdom of five comedians, who are all putting their reputations on the line. Alex Horne assists Davies in setting up unique and ridiculous tasks for the competitors.

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: The fresh batch of teams face the now-iconic secret challenge and this time, it's the classic Paris Brest, with a twist. For their next challenge, the teams create childhood-toy-inspired showpieces concealing crumble and custard desserts.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Newcastle, first up is 25-year-old barista Delphinium, who's eager to prove that vegetarians can win with her travel inspired menu.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the second night in Newcastle, art teacher Karen serves up a ginger-themed menu, with each course including her favourite spice and art class entertainment.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the third day in Newcastle, mobile hairdresser and amateur songstress Debbie plans a bit of showbiz razzle dazzle, but there's a shock in store.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the fourth day in Newcastle, 64-year-old David plans an elaborate Ibizan-style menu of tapas and paella but has he bitten off more than he can chew?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's the final night in and around Newcastle. Can Essex boy Mark impress his new Geordie chums with deep fried brie, slow cooked lamb and passion fruit pavlova?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: This new competition from the Nottingham/Derby area kicks off at American Hali's house where she's hoping to impress her guests with a travel inspired menu.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Second to host in Nottingham and Derby area is hosted by 28-year-old car salesman Alistair. He's hoping to step the competition up a gear and speed away with the £1000 prize.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Nottingham, fun-loving funeral arranger Kim is going all out to win by hosting an evening packed with games galore and a mixed bag menu.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In this competition from the Nottingham and Derby area, it's 34-year-old customer relations associate and part-time drag queen Adam to host. He is hoping his theatrical night of flamboyance will win him the £1000 cash prize.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's the final night of the competition and 57-year-old Lesley is counting on a night of glamour and glitz - plus some good home-cooked food - to win her the prize.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Newcastle, there's wine snobbery, a fairy-tale feast and a Moroccan menu based solely on one couple's love of hummus.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: In London, will Jody and Shane's million-pound budget be enough for a slice of the Big Smoke? And Kirstie gives foodies Roopal and Hershil all the ingredients for an ideal home.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman helps Keith and Heidi find their dream holiday home in southern Alicante in Spain, with enough space for their four children, six grandchildren and two dogs to visit. She lines up five great properties for their budget of £170,000.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Engaged couple Sharon and Paul from Shropshire would love to own a holiday home in the seaside town of Gran Alacant on Spain's southern Costa Blanca. Jean Johansson shows them what £135k can buy.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Leah Charles-King helps Julie and Leo find the perfect holiday home on the southern Costa Blanca for all three generations of their family to enjoy. With a maximum budget of £120,000, Leah shows them five impressive properties.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Paul dreams of owning a holiday home on Spain's Costa Blanca, to escape the winter months and spend quality time with family. Ben Hillman shows him what £80k can buy, but are any the 'one'?

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: In London, will Jody and Shane's million-pound budget be enough for a slice of the Big Smoke? And Kirstie gives foodies Roopal and Hershil all the ingredients for an ideal home.

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Greg and Georgie plan to convert a cavernous, dilapidated, 35-year-old barn in Georgie's parents' garden in Kent into a peaceful safe haven. But budget-squeezing building work soon sets in.

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Can nervous Jack find common ground with Rachella? Scott plucks up the courage to shed his drag alter-ego, Casta Hex. And can DJ Priscilla and accountant Raj reconcile their musical differences?

programme.title: Dante's Peak
programme.desc: Dante's Peak has just been pronounced the second best place in the world to live. Will this title be affected by the danger of the volcano which looms over it? Volcanologist Harry Dalton comes to the town to study the volcano, which he believes to be about to blow. His only ally is the town's mayor, Rachel Wando, whose family is one of those at risk.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britain's sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the week's biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: In the Footsteps of Killers
programme.desc: Was murdered Wolverhampton sex worker Janine the victim of a serial killer?

programme.title: Emergency
programme.desc: Emergency returns as a tense four-part serial across the week. As a pedestrian is crushed by a bus and a holiday paraglide goes wrong, can the frontline trauma units save lives?

programme.title: Jamie's One-Pan Wonders
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver shows us how to make a foolproof traybaked salmon, super-easy tomato fritters, and roasted rolled pork with a risotto that are both cooked in the same casserole pan.

programme.title: Winter Road Rescue
programme.desc: Shot over three months, this documentary series follows the snowplough drivers and patrols who work around the clock to make sure the UK does not grind to a halt when winter weather hits. In the Highlands, snowplough teams battle blizzards, ice, flooding and landslides to keep the roads open, whilst across the UK patrols fight snow, gales and icy conditions to reach motorists stranded in the cold.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: It is a few day before Christmas, but Jason is short of festive cheer when a yuletide party becomes a full-blown riot. Adam and Nick uncover a drunk mother driving her daughter to the shops for some present-buying and Kev is seconds from disaster when he fearlessly prevents a car from becoming a fireball.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Scott and Adam discover a huge stash in the boot of a car, and a runaway dealer just misses mowing down Gary. The crime-fighting spree continues when Ady collars a car thief and the interceptors are called to a pub fight on New Year's Eve. Throw in five cars pursuing a runaway on a busy motorway and you are in for quite a ride.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Scott and Dave track a pair of mobile phone thieves with the help of GPS technology, Nick pushes his motor to the limit as he pursues a criminal gang and Jason has to deal with his own personal traffic nightmare. Elsewhere, Vicky spots a van overflowing with suspicious-looking scrap and a BMW ploughs into a house over the Christmas period.

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Britain's Speeders
programme.desc: PCs Ian and Matt pull over a Chevvy. A search of the vehicle reveals drugs paraphernalia. In Warrington, cops the hunt for an uninsured driver who may be drunk.

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Britain's Speeders
programme.desc: Northumbria Police deal with an angry van driver who has no MOT. And a PC faces a suspected drug driver whose vocal boyfriend won't come quietly.

programme.desc: Set in the Aegean Sea and extolling the Special Boat Service. programme.title: Air Force One
programme.desc: High-altitude thriller in which the presidential aircraft, Air Force One, is hijacked by Russian communist radicals demanding the release of the Kazakhstani leader. With the First Lady and her family trapped on board, the hijackers threaten to kill one hostage every half-hour until their demands are met. What they don't know is that the president himself is hiding in the hold and formulating a plan to save his family.

programme.desc: Taichung Men's Prison is the biggest in Taiwan, housing some of the most dangerous criminals and operating a system of progressive correctional treatment.

programme.desc: Game Wardens search for the elusive big horn sheep in the mountains of West Texas and they track down a bow hunter, incriminated by his own game camera.

programme.title: Dr. Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Dr Jeff helps Lola, who needs major surgery on her kidneys, and Dr Amy meets Bobo, who is badly wounded after a cat fight. Meanwhile, animal control brings in a neglected dog named Juicy.

programme.title: Dr. Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Dr Jeff performs surgery on Slash the dog who can't keep his food down and weighs 20-lbs less than he should. Plus, Winter the cat has a bloody wound on his side.

programme.title: Pit Bulls and Parolees
programme.desc: On the streets of New Orleans, the VRC crew is on a mission to catch a runaway dog. A Californian family wants a new furry family member, a paralysed pup named Polly takes a trip to the vet, and Mariah pitches a fundraising idea to the parolees.

programme.title: My Cat From Hell
programme.desc: A pet psychic can't communicate with her own vicious cat Lily.

programme.title: My Cat From Hell
programme.desc: Debbie finds herself torn between her boyfriend and her peeing cat Blinkie.

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete builds a treehouse for the children of injured Marines. Cargo netting provides an exciting way to enter, with more nets making a bridge between sections.

programme.title: The Dark Side of the Sun
programme.desc: Scientists have only recently begun to understand the true nature of our 'local star', the Sun. This programme discusses its past and future, and its effect on our planet.

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: New science and amazing demonstrations reveal how a lizard can literally walk on water. And, can jellyfish live forever?

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: A bird expert reveals how a barn owl catches prey it can't even see. And, what makes octopuses masters of disguise?

programme.title: Extinct or Alive
programme.desc: Forrest scours lakes and rivers of both metropolitan and rural Vietnam in search of one of the last living specimens of the giant softshell turtle.

programme.title: Edge of Alaska
programme.desc: Neil Darish is laying low after announcing his plan to develop the motherlode mine into a mine tour to encourage tourists to visit McCarthy.

programme.title: Tanked

programme.title: Tanked

programme.title: Edge of Alaska
programme.desc: Neil defies both nature and locals by developing his mine tour, but the town discovers his secret project. Plus, tragedy forces one of McCarthy's best to fight for their life.

programme.title: Anna Haugh's Big Irish Food Tour
programme.desc: The 1985 Black Ball Final made snooker legend Dennis Taylor a household name, and there's nowhere that celebrates this giant of sport more than his native Tyrone. Anna visits him at the snooker hall where it all began. Dennis can still remember the soda bread his grandmother used to make over an open fire. Can Anna recreate this childhood recipe?The pair then join fishermen on the largest lake in the UK to celebrate one of Ireland's most unique delicacies - the Lough Neagh eel.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

Taking Donkey along, they set off for Far Far Away to visit them, but the trip is hampered by the jealousy of a wicked fairy godmother and her hired assassin, Puss in Boots.

programme.title: UEFA Champions League Football
programme.desc: Highlights of the 2026 UEFA Champions League final between Arsenal and PSG in Budapest. Twenty years on from their last appearance in the European Cup final, Arsenal are seeking to win their first Champions League title. However, they face a formidable test in the reigning European champions, Paris Saint-Germain. Jason Mohammad presents, with expert analysis from Nedum Onuoha and Matt Upson. Commentary by Steve Wilson and Chris Sutton.

programme.title: The Repair Shop: On the Road
programme.desc: Will and Dom learn how a devastating fire put a 5,000-year-old craft in jeopardy, Chris Shaw repairs a Gaelic pipe music book, and Dom has the chance to make his own sporran.

programme.title: Icons of Football
programme.desc: A portrait of Julie Fleeting, who became Scotland's all-time top scorer and an Arsenal legend while balancing life as a mum and PE teacher.

programme.title: Highland Cops
programme.desc: Police are alerted to the discovery of human remains near Aviemore. They suspect this might be the last resting place of Rodrigo Falcon, a popular barman who went missing in December 2022. Sergeant Stephen Todd and his team confront a woman who is keeping monkeys and tropical animals in a ruined house in freezing cold Caithness. In Inverness, PC Siobhan Cooper faces rowdy bank holiday crowds.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Ken forces Richard to accompany him to Aviemore so that he can help him navigate the QR code scanner at the train station ticket barrier. However, a mix-up on the platform turns what should be a simple day trip into an almighty fiasco.

programme.title: Pitch Invasion: How the Scottish and Irish Changed Football
programme.desc: How managers and players like David Moyes and Charlie Nicholas dealt with the highs and lows of English football. While Charlie Nicholas made headlines off the pitch in the 1980s, David Moyes saw glory turn to failure in 2014.

programme.title: TRNSMT
programme.desc: Shereen Cutkelvin presents highlights from TRNSMT 2025, including performances from Wet Leg, James Bay, Jake Bugg, Fontaines DC, Jade, Biffy Clyro and Snow Patrol.

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: When garden space is limited, or just for the joy of growing your own, having an allotment is the perfect pastime. Starting in Calum's allotment in Leven, we visit the other allotment holders in the Beechgrove team.

programme.title: The Town That Floored the World
programme.desc: Tracing the history of linoleum to its origins in the mid 19th century and examining how the Scottish port town of Kirkcaldy become the world centre for its manufacture. Current and former linoleum workers share their stories of a life in flooring, and linoleum's role in high art and design is also explored.

programme.title: Wild Cameramen at Work
programme.desc: Cameramen Doug Anderson and Doug Allan reveal the technical challenges of filming under the sea and encounter animals as diverse as blue whales, white-tip sharks and the weedy sea dragon performing a graceful courtship dance.

programme.title: The Three Kings
programme.desc: Rising from industrial cities, Matt Busby, Bill Shankly and Jock Stein each became legendary football managers. Winning over the hearts of the fans, explore how they changed the face of the game.

programme.title: Still Game
programme.desc: While Isa gets into a fix with some cute little animals, Jack and Victor decide to stop putting things off and go on that adventure they have been planning for years. A new month rolls round and Jack is excited to see what new picture he will reveal on his calendar, but tensions quickly rise as Victor tries to meddle in his fun.

programme.title: Scot Squad
programme.desc: Traffic Officer Hugh McKirdy becomes starstruck as he and his partner Surjit Singh respond to an incident involving Michelle McManus.

programme.title: TUNE
programme.desc: Addressing the lack of nightlife for young people in the area, Ullapool native Sigi and his friend Jemima take on the formidable challenge of creating Scotland's most remote club night.

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The Doctor is on the most beautiful train in history speeding among the stars of the future, but a deadly creature is stalking the passengers.

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Miranda doesn't know how to cope when all the men in her life suddenly turn their affections towards her.

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Miranda and Stevie compete to be best friends with the new waitress at the restaurant, Tamara. The trouble is Tamara is 22 and they both find keeping up with an energetic 20 something surprisingly tiring. Their age becomes an issue after an art class, followed by all night karaoke, followed by a swimming experience that puts Miranda face to face with her nudity issues.

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: There is a storm blowing in Finglas and it claims its first victim when the pipes in Winnie and Sharon's house burst. Thank goodness Mrs Brown is on hand to offer a roof over their heads, but before long there is a storm brewing in the Brown household too, as Agnes and Cathy discover living with best friends isn't the picnic they had imagined.

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: There's bad news for Winnie when Agnes discovers Peggy Piper the Pernod poisoner is out on appeal and could be coming back to Finglas. This could mean Winnie has to give evidence against her again as she was the one who saw Peggy poison her husband.

programme.title: Are You There God? It's Me, Margaret.
programme.desc: Eleven-year-old Margaret moves to a new town and starts to contemplate everything about life, friendship and adolescence. She relies on her mother, who offers loving support, and her grandmother, who's coming to terms with finding happiness.

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: It's that time of year again - the Mr Teesside Beauty Pageant, and the grand prize is £20,000, but someone foul lurks in the shadows to attempt to fix the competition.

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: Dickie's been booked for a drag gig and has invited a load of agents in the hope of making it to the big time, while Mam faces a ghost from her past.

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Now that Gaz and Donna are divorced, Gaz decides it is time to propose to Janet. Scared that she is losing all her friends, Donna helps him organise the proposal, to the disapproval of Tim who worries she's holding on to the past. Elsewhere, Wesley gives Janet a job on the market stall, but she suffers from a stomach ache. When the pain gets so bad, she is rushed to hospital.

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Janet arrives back from hospital with Wesley and Tim, but there is no sign of Gaz. Later, when he finds out that she has spent the night with Wesley holding her hand he blurts out that he has slept with Donna, so Janet throws him out. Elsewhere, Louise tells Tim she is desperate for a boyfriend, just as ex-boyband member Scott enters the pub.

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: An unexpected social media meltdown forces Geoffrey to reassess his life and values.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Max confronts the Mitchells about his suspicions, Gina gives Eddie the cold shoulder, and Zack gets some unwelcome news.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Grant steps up to protect his family, Zack purposely raises suspicions, and Eddie and Harry clash.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: It's the day of Vicki and Ross's wedding, but will it go ahead? Priya gets a shock.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Chaos erupts at Vicki and Ross's wedding, and Denise receives some news.

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Pip attempts to return to normal life and focus on her friends, school and Ravi. But when a key witness in Max Hastings's trial disappears, Pip feels the pull of a new case.

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Pip hits a dead end and rethinks. Digging into Jamie's recent behaviour and last movements, she realises he may not be an innocent victim after all.

programme.title: Arthur the King
programme.desc: Michael Light and a team of athletes compete in the Adventure Racing World Championship. As they get pushed to the outer limits of endurance, a dog named Arthur comes along for the ride, redefining what victory, loyalty and friendship truly means.

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Stacey heads to Bedfordshire to the private site where 23-year-old Chantelle lives with her husband, Swayley. Chantelle prides herself in keeping with the old Gypsy values her granny Rita taught her and sharing them on social media.

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: In Britain the shoplifting is at an all-time high, incidents of violence and abuse against shop staff have skyrocketed, and a third of Brits have admitted to stealing through the self-checkouts. To understand what's causing this high-street epidemic, Stacey is meeting the people behind the crime and spending time on the shop floor with the staff who deal with shoplifting every day. In Manchester city centre, beauty and cosmetics chain Bodycare has one of its biggest and most shoplifted stores.

programme.title: Just Act Normal
programme.desc: Tiana, Tionne, and Tanika try to live life under the radar after their mum seemingly disappears, but when her drug dealer arrives demanding money, their secret risks being exposed.

programme.title: Just Act Normal
programme.desc: The siblings' secret's exposed. Tiana plans their escape but fails and is forced to confront their estranged dad. Feelgood offers to help, but there's a catch - he wants to move in.

programme.title: The Trials of Pa Salieu
programme.desc: After prison, Coventry musician Pa Salieu returns with renewed purpose and takes the stage at Glastonbury. While he fights to rebuild his career, can music save him again?

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: A desolate train track seems the perfect spot to end it all, until someone else turns up with the same idea. Awkward.

programme.desc: An ex-con who learned to play guitar in prison becomes an egomaniac on his rise to the top of the recording industry.

programme.sub-title: Final: Wigan Warriors v St. Helens
programme.desc: Wigan Warriors face St Helens in the final of the 2026 Women's Challenge Cup.

programme.title: Nicholas and Alexandra
programme.desc: When Tsar Nicholas weds the German princess Alexandra, the marriage proves unpopular with the Russian people, a situation not improved when she has four daughters. When she finally bears a son, the infant's acute haemophilia can only be controlled by the powers of the fanatical monk Rasputin.

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: On a remote island in the Canadian Arctic, a pair of white gyrfalcons and a pack of arctic wolves are struggling to raise their young. As the falcons' eggs hatch, the parents must find enough arctic hares to feed them. The wolf pack are raising their cubs in a hillside den.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin is at Ickworth House, an Italianate palace in the Suffolk countryside, and Adam Partridge and Will Axon assist in the valuations.

programme.title: Hidden Treasures of the National Trust
programme.desc: Preserving the legacy of St Cuthbert and a colony of breeding seabirds, on the remote Farne Islands. And in Wales, experts race to save a fragile bible that shaped Welsh culture.

programme.title: Lucy Worsley Investigates
programme.desc: Lucy explores how the battle for American independence wasn't only fought on American soil but also unfolded in Britain itself, with sabotage, espionage, and political unrest reshaping the empire.

programme.title: Boxing LIVE
programme.sub-title: Super Lightweight Bout: Adam Azim v Steve Claggett
programme.desc: Live boxing as Adam Azim makes his return from injury against Steve Claggett.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: A look back at some of the songs performed on the show in 1993. Dance music and Euro pop continued to dominate the charts with performances from Haddaway, Sub Sub, Ace of Base and Robin S. The early stages of Britpop began to emerge with Suede and Radiohead plus Icelandic superstar Björk makes an early appearance. Then there are the big sellers from Meat Loaf and Gabrielle and the grooves came courtesy of Jamiroquai and Chaka Demus & Pliers plus many more.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Hear the best tracks from the chart, interviews with chart artists, and gossip from the music world.

programme.title: Everything Went Fine
programme.desc: When her elderly father has a stroke, Emmanuelle rushes to his bedside. Sick and half-paralysed in his hospital bed, he asks Emmanuelle to help him end his life.

programme.title: Death Valley
programme.desc: Following the death of a chef in an arson attack, John and Janie arrive at a small fishing village to scrutinise the locals.

programme.title: Dear England
programme.desc: With the England team at an all-time low after an embarrassing defeat to Iceland, Gareth Southgate becomes the new manager. Haunted by his own penalty miss with England, he inherits a broken team and a jaded fanbase. With psychologist Pippa Grange, he sets about changing the team's mindset and tackling his and England's biggest fear - penalties.

programme.desc: The bidders journey to the Dallas suburb of Waxahachie, Ricky and Bubba score some green, Mary's scandalous find could earn her a huge profit and Marry gets a buzz from a hand-powered machine.

programme.title: Storage Wars Texas

programme.desc: The bidders make a return trip to the city of Waxahachie, Vic and his protege meet Jamie Benn of the Dallas Stars, Ricky and Bubba sail to Houston and Jenny is touched by something angelic.

programme.desc: The buyers visit the city of Mesquite on one of the hottest days of the year, Vic's protege takes a hit and Mary's discovery leads her to a llama farm.

programme.title: Storage Wars Texas

programme.title: The UnXplained With William Shatner
programme.desc: Genius is widely considered to be the ability to change the world in an incredible way like inventing new technology, deciphering the mysteries of the universe, or creating transcendent art; but it's origin is a mystery.

programme.title: The UnXplained With William Shatner
programme.desc: More than 80% of the Earth's oceans remain unexplored, and in many ways, the underwater world is the final frontier. As new technology allows us to explore oceans and deep lakes more than ever before, what will we find?

programme.title: World's Most Unexplained
programme.desc: Exploring events that defy rational and scientific explanations.

programme.title: World's Most Unexplained

programme.title: World's Most Unexplained

programme.sub-title: Crocodile Monster, Tiktok USO, and Ghost Dog Collar Removal

programme.title: What on Earth?

programme.title: What on Earth?

programme.title: Evidence of the Unexplained

programme.title: Love Monster

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops

programme.desc: When Wilson finds out he's the first of his kind, his friends deem him Chuggington Royalty, a title that quickly goes to his engine.

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Evil Pea causes chaos in the veggie aisle with a trolley loaded to the brim with stinky cheese. Supertato and Broccoli must use the Tato Car to get rid of the stink.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Flop is pushing Bing on the swing in the park when Pando arrives and wants to have a go too. They agree to let each other have turns by counting up to ten, but when Pando has his turn he won't count properly.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing and his friends are making leaf pictures in the woods. They take a piece of paper, place it on a leaf and rub a crayon onto it. They each choose their own leaf, but Bing's is whisked away by the wind! Luckily, he manages to catch it.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: All the Squirrels are having a fine old time playing with the hose. All except one - Tag. His feet hurt. Poor Tag. 'Why do my feet hurt?', he asks Duggee. While Duggee has a think, he tells them 'why' is a great question.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: It's news time at the Clubhouse, and the Squirrels share their exciting news of the week with each other. Happy got a new bath sponge, Norrie is growing a sunflower and Roly, after thinking hard if he's got any news at all, remembers he's getting a new brother or sister. The Squirrels have all sorts of questions to ask.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: It's the yearly G.O. Challenge, a labyrinth full of trials designed to test the Go Jet cadets's geographical knowledge. The Go Jetters are amazed to discover that the reigning champion is Glitc, but only because he sacrificed his teammates in order to win! Entering the maze, the Go Jetters must face challenges related to the continents, the weather and recycling, all while trying to beat Glitch's record.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.sub-title: The Tale of Jeremy Fisher's Musical Adventure
programme.desc: Peter embarks on a series of exciting adventures and tries to be just like his late father, with the help of his best friends Benjamin and Lily. Jeremy Fisher gets into trouble while composing a new piece. Only Peter and his friends can save him!

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: The Octonauts have trouble getting past a huge gang of hippos on their way back from a mission on the African river.

programme.title: Vida the Vet
programme.desc: Zigzag's ADHD gives her a case of the hops, so Vida teaches her how to make a to-do list.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Nori and Dot are stuck in a tree house without a door. How can Mojo and Bo get them out? By sending the treehouse flying across town, that's how!

programme.title: Joe Wicks Activate
programme.desc: Can you complete every level of my Gamer Challenge? This one is all about gaming. So, get a good run-up, and let us jump those platforms together. Level one is runners, two is squats, then three is burpees! The boss battle is the Activate!

programme.title: Something Special: We're All Friends
programme.desc: Today, Mr Tumble is looking for a hobby. Meanwhile, Justin meets a friend who plays Powerchair football as his hobby.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bingo and Bluey get stuck on a question while playing a game of grannies. They call Nana to find out whether grannies can dance.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Indy thinks she's no good because her model horse looks like a cow, but Calypso encourages her not to give up, so she tries to fix it with a little help from Winton.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: As Peggee and the Hummingbirds journey up the river to deliver a parcel, they have fun spotting and avoiding the wildlife that they meet along the way. All while Otti tries to avoid playing with the boat whistle!

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.sub-title: The Days of the Week Badge
programme.desc: The Squirrels are excited to learn that Mr Wobbly, the jelly juggler, is coming to town, and they can't wait to see him. When they become impatient, Duggee explains to them that the week will fly by and that there's lots to be done in that time.

programme.title: Zog

programme.title: Rafi the Wishing Wizard

The cheeky gingerbreads dash through the café, splattering icing and riding on Deborah's back as they try to reach Gingerbread Land. With Jake's help, Rafi traps them under a cake dome. Dash reminds her that their messy look is what makes them special.

programme.title: Weasy Family
programme.desc: Weasy hits his head and loses his memory! Can the Ducklings help him remember them?

programme.desc: Lizard's monster plant takes over the city. When Doc Ock captures the Spider CRAWL-R while on a mission in space, Miles and his mum, Rio, work together to free their ship.

programme.title: Stan Can

programme.title: Supertato

programme.title: Small Potatoes
programme.desc: In this swinging homage to the Sinatra era, the Small Potatoes sing about how they're no average spuds.

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: While rescuing river creatures after a storm, the Octonauts find a strange egg and come under attack by stinging platypuses.

programme.title: Vida the Vet
programme.desc: When Otto's feet smell like cheese, Vida knows just how to help. Everyone is happy about that except little mouse Sunny-he loves cheese!

programme.title: Go Jetters

programme.title: Rafi the Wishing Wizard

It's exciting at first, but when the wish ends, everything disappears. Rafi feels sad, until Doris shares a story about planting an apple tree when she was little. Rafi realises that nature likes to take its time, and that waiting can bring something special.

programme.title: Yukee

Parents, carers and their little ones at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too. The What's in the Box? game reveals a happy face and a sad face, and when the toddlers and parents open up a bigger box, they discover lots more objects and toys that are inspired by feelings. This theme is explored through play, song and story.

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: During a game of I Spy, Ginger spots something that none of the Peas can see.

They're so yummy that Mum gets the hiccups. Later, Eden and Ava build a den for the whole family to hide in. They imagine they're camping in the woods and look out at the stars. But will they be safe from the noisy tiger in the forest? Roar!

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Ross and Rachel Take a Break
programme.desc: Rachel's job prevents her from celebrating her anniversary with Ross. Wanting more time to focus on her new job, she suggests the two take a break from their relationship. Convinced that Rachel has dumped him for one of her workmates, Ross hooks up with a woman from the copy shop that Joey and Chandler have been after.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross tries to prevent Rachel from learning his disturbing secret. Meanwhile, Monica, Phoebe, Chandler and Joey spend a night eavesdropping.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Without the Ski Trip
programme.desc: Ross and Rachel have been fighting for the group's attention since the break up. Rachel invites everyone except Ross to her family cabin in the mountains, but on the way to the cabin, they run out of petrol. Everyone wants to call Ross for help, but Rachel won't let them.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Hypnosis Tape
programme.desc: Phoebe is upset when her 18-year-old half-brother, Frank Jr, wants to marry his attractive home economics teacher, who is more than 25 years older than him. Elsewhere, Monica receives a tip from an admirer worth a small fortune, and a hypnosis tape intended to assist Chandler give up smoking has a rather strange effect on him.

programme.title: Utopia
programme.desc: Set inside the offices of the Nation Building Authority, a newly created government organisation responsible for overseeing major infrastructure projects, Utopia explores that moment when bureaucracy and grand dreams collide.

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Show

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.sub-title: The Best Bits of Mr Bean
programme.desc: Loveable buffoon Mr Bean has the unique talent of turning the most ordinary of situations into moments of excruciating embarrassment.

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.sub-title: Hair by Mr. Bean of London
programme.desc: Mr Bean has a grand day at the hairdresser's, enters Teddy in a pet show, and has to find his way off a train when he loses his ticket.

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: Mr Bean has a hard time waiting his turn at the hospital, later he visits Windsor Castle and, that night, has trouble with a bit of insomnia.

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: Mr Bean goes to the launderette where he loses his trousers once again. He then plays a round of mini golf but has trouble keeping his ball on the green.

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: Mr Bean treats himself to a weekend at a posh hotel where he manages to get himself into one embarrassing situation after another.

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: Mr Bean goes Christmas shopping, conducts a Salvation Army band, and has Christmas dinner with his long-suffering girlfriend.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Tiny T-Shirt
programme.desc: Rachel's former colleague Mark asks her out on a date. When Rachel invites Ross to her apartment, he thinks she wants to get back together, but she just wants to return his things. Later, as Rachel and Mark return from their date, Ross spies on them through the peephole of Joey and Chandler's door. Meanwhile, Monica dates a millionaire, and Joey is cast in a play and clashes with the female lead.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monica inherits a beautiful dollhouse that is too elaborate to play with, so Phoebe makes her own, but it meets a sad end. Meanwhile, Joey thinks he's found his true love, and Rachel's boss dates Chandler, placing her in a dangerous situation.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With a Chick and a Duck
programme.desc: When Monica's millionaire friend Pete buys a restaurant and asks Monica to be the head chef, she is faced with a difficult decision. Meanwhile, Ross makes a sacrifice for Rachel, and Joey buys Chandler an unusual pet.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: The friends go to the opening night of one of Joey's plays. When Rachel finds out that Ross is bringing a date, she finds one of her own. Ross tries to convince Rachel that her date is mentally unbalanced. Meanwhile, Joey's play gets terrible reviews, but his co-star provides some comfort.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross develops a skin growth that doctors can't identify.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Ultimate Fighting Champion
programme.desc: Monica finds herself dating a guy who wants to be the Ultimate Fighting Champion. Meanwhile, Chandler finds his male boss's attentions just a little too friendly, and the group overhear a scandalous conversation at Central Perk.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe invites everyone to stay at her massage client's beach house for the weekend. Phoebe meets someone who knows details about her family, Rachel expresses her jealousy of Ross' uninhibited girlfriend, and Joey nags the friends into agreeing to play a party game that requires the participants to gradually take off items of clothing.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross is dating Rachel again but hates what he had to do to get back together, while Phoebe and her real mother, Phoebe Snr, get to know each other better. Meanwhile, Monica is badly stung by a jellyfish and has to resort to drastic measures to relieve the pain.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Chandler and Joey lose all their furniture, Monica finds that one date with her high-school heartthrob is more than enough, and Ross plays tricks on Rachel following their break-up. Elsewhere, Phoebe is temporarily convinced that a cat is the reincarnation of her dead adoptive mother Lily.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Chandler has a brief fling with Rachel's boss Joanna which leaves him handcuffed without his trousers in her office. Meanwhile, Joey can only afford one book in an encyclopaedia set, and Monica caters a party for her mother.

programme.title: New Girl
programme.desc: Newly-single Jess arrives at her new flat with an unusual line in house-warming gifts, including a `Dirty Dancing' DVD and a neat line in personal theme tunes.

programme.title: New Girl
programme.desc: Coach moves out and Winston returns to his old flat. Winston, Schmidt and Nick refuse to lend Jess any household items so that she has to go to her ex-boyfriend Spencer's house to collect her belongings. Unfortunately, Jess ends up doing her ex and his new girlfriend a favour. Undeterred, Nick encourages Jess to stand up to Spencer so that she can move on in her life.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: The off-colour adventures of a group of foul-mouthed elementary school boys in South Park, Colorado. The students, including, Eric Cartman, Stan Marsh, Kyle Broflovski and Kenny McCormick target every conceivable group in outrageous ways.

programme.sub-title: The One With Joey's New Girlfriend

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Dirty Girl

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Chandler Crosses the Line

programme.title: Friends

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Ross Hugs Rachel
programme.desc: Joey is upset to learn that Chandler plans to move out to live with Monica, but Rachel seems strangely unaffected by the news. Meanwhile, Phoebe pleads with Ross to either get an annulment or admit that he still loves Rachel.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monica and Chandler disagree over how to redecorate Rachel's old room, and despite Phoebe's admonitions, Ross makes a drastic decision about Rachel.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Joey Loses His Insurance
programme.desc: After learning that his health insurance has lapsed because he has not worked enough as an actor, Joey suffers a painful hernia, and Rachel finally finds out that Ross never annulled their marriage.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel forces Ross to terminate their marriage, Phoebe's first triplet babysitting experience proves stressful, and Joey pretends to own a German sports car.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One on the Last Night
programme.desc: It's the last night before Chandler moves into Monica's apartment, leaving Joey alone and prompting Rachel to make plans to move in with Phoebe.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe's lack of coordination while jogging embarrasses Rachel, and Joey gets a sexy new roommate.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Chandler doesn't want new flatmate Janine to make the apartment too `girly', Ross overdoses on tooth whitener before a date, and Phoebe tells Rachel that she kissed Ralph Lauren at Rachel's workplace.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Ross Got High
programme.desc: On Thanksgiving, Chandler tries to gain favour with Monica's parents Jack and Judy, but an old secret involving Ross causes problems that he is unaware of. Meanwhile, Rachel proves she is no domestic goddess when she creates a quite disastrous dessert.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Apothecary Table
programme.desc: Joey is on cloud nine when he starts to date Janine, but she doesn't like his friends, and Rachel worries that Phoebe won't appreciate the way she furnishes their apartment.

programme.title: New Girl
programme.desc: The gang are invited to a wedding of a mutual friend. Nick is worried that he'll run into Caroline, his ex, and so asks Jess to be his pretend girlfriend. Meanwhile, Schmidt is on the prowl for single women but ends up with his sexual arch-enemy and Winston relishes his role as usher by getting competitive on the dance-floor.

programme.title: New Girl
programme.desc: Jess accidentally walks in on a naked Nick and ruins his self-confidence before a date with a co-worker. Meanwhile, Winston busies himself on Wikipedia.

programme.title: Cruise TV with LoveitBookit

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Princess Leia Fantasy
programme.desc: Monica is distraught after her break-up with Richard. Phoebe unsuccessfully tries to raise her spirits, but ultimately it's Mr Geller who comforts his daughter. Elsewhere, Chandler and Janice begin seeing each other again and Joey can't stand it. The two plan a day of fun to try to overcome Joey's aversions for Chandler's sake. Meanwhile, Rachel tells Phoebe and Monica about Ross's Princess Leia fantasy.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where No One's Ready
programme.desc: In a race against the clock, Ross tries to get the gang dressed and ready for a black-tie benefit at the museum.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monica decides the best way to get over Richard is to keep herself busy. She develops a `jam plan', hoping the distraction of cooking will divert her attention. After making jars and jars of jam Monica decides she still wants to have a baby, so she begins searching for a sperm donor, and comes across Joey's file. Elsewhere, Phoebe is mistakenly stalked by a man who thinks she's Ursula, and Chandler receives dating advice.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Metaphorical Tunnel
programme.desc: Ross panics when he sees his son with a Barbie doll, and Chandler's fear of commitment surfaces in his relationship with Janice.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe's attempts to introduce her visiting half brother Frank Jr to the delights of city life leave him thinking that she works in a brothel. Meanwhile, Ross lists five famous women that he would like to date. To his surprise, actress Isabella Rossellini, one of the women he rejected while narrowing down his list, pops in to Central Perk.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: When Janice asks whether any of the friends have ever dated one another the gang has a flashback to three years ago (one year before the pilot was supposed to have happened) when everyone's lives were beginning to come together. Phoebe moves out of Monica's apartment, Chandler searches for a new roommate - ending up with Joey, Monica bumps into Rachel, and Ross learns that Carol is a lesbian.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Race Car Bed
programme.desc: When Monica buys a new bed from the Mattress King, the wrong one is delivered. Meanwhile, Joey sees Janice kissing her husband, and Rachel's father clashes with Ross.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Giant Poking Device
programme.desc: Chandler and Janice reach a turning point in their relationship, while Monica accidentally injures Ben and tries to hide it from Ross.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: On Thanksgiving, the friends form teams and play American football. Ross and Monica relive their sibling rivalry by going head-to-head as opposing team captains. Rachel is upset that Ross didn't choose her for his team, and Joey tries to outwit his foe Chandler. Meanwhile, the turkey is left stewing while the losing team calls for a rematch.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel wonders if she'll ever get another job after she quits Central Perk, Joey's new job selling Christmas trees upsets Phoebe, and Ross accidentally breaks a little girl's leg.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Chandler Can't Remember Which Sister
programme.desc: Ross is suspicious about Rachel's possible job offer from a good-looking stranger, while Chandler can't remember which of Joey's many attractive sisters he kissed at a party.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With All the Jealousy
programme.desc: Ross becomes increasingly jealous of Rachel's sexy new colleague, Mark, and gets the wrong end of the stick when he spies on Rachel at her office. Meanwhile, Monica is enamoured with a sexy busboy who writes love poems for her, and Joey gets himself into trouble by faking his CV in order to get a part in a Broadway musical.

programme.title: The Jewel of the Nile
programme.desc: Jack and romance writer Joan are once again fighting like cats and dogs. However, when Joan is imprisoned by a tyrannical dictator while travelling in Africa, Jack rushes to rescue her. Together, they cross northern Africa - accompanied by Ralph.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: Bradley and Barney Walsh's epic road trip continues - in this episode, the Walshes have reached Guatemala, where they pick up a new mode of transport that quickly becomes a nightmare for Bradley to drive.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh is not interested in 'travelling the world' like his son Barney, who despite being considerably younger than him, has visited everywhere from Indonesia to San Francisco and Moscow.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Monica and Richard Are Just Friends
programme.desc: Monica bumps into Richard in a video store and they decide to work on their friendship, but the more time they spend together, the more sexual their relationship becomes. Meanwhile, Phoebe dates an athlete who unintentionally keeps exposing himself by wearing loose shorts, and Rachel agrees to read `The Shining' if Joey will read `Little Women'.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe is reunited with her former singing partner, Leslie, a failed television commercial jingle writer who wants to use Phoebe's signature song `Smelly Cat' in an advertisement for cat litter. Meanwhile, Ross becomes jealous when Rachel agrees to go to a fashion lecture with Mark, a former colleague who has just left for a new job, and Chandler meets a beautiful woman, Ginger, who has a prosthetic leg.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Ross and Rachel Take a Break
programme.desc: Rachel's job prevents her from celebrating her anniversary with Ross. Wanting more time to focus on her new job, she suggests the two take a break from their relationship. Convinced that Rachel has dumped him for one of her workmates, Ross hooks up with a woman from the copy shop that Joey and Chandler have been after.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross tries to prevent Rachel from learning his disturbing secret. Meanwhile, Monica, Phoebe, Chandler and Joey spend a night eavesdropping.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Without the Ski Trip
programme.desc: Ross and Rachel have been fighting for the group's attention since the break up. Rachel invites everyone except Ross to her family cabin in the mountains, but on the way to the cabin, they run out of petrol. Everyone wants to call Ross for help, but Rachel won't let them.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Hypnosis Tape
programme.desc: Phoebe is upset when her 18-year-old half-brother, Frank Jr, wants to marry his attractive home economics teacher, who is more than 25 years older than him. Elsewhere, Monica receives a tip from an admirer worth a small fortune, and a hypnosis tape intended to assist Chandler give up smoking has a rather strange effect on him.

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: Michael McIntyre hosts as Big Narstie, Ellie Taylor, Frankie Bridge, Harry Judd, Jonathan Ross, Richie Anderson and Vogue Williams try to help the contestants answer their way to a fortune.

programme.title: Kevin & Perry Go Large
programme.desc: Television comedy spin-off about two desperate teenagers who travel to Ibiza to expose their musical talent to a wider audience and, more importantly, to lose their virginity. Despite having adult supervision along for the ride, the pair soon cross paths with a world famous DJ and spot the girls of their dreams.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: A topical panel show based on a huge series of opinion polls carried out around Britain. These `Cat Polls' are primed to root out the important issues of the day, like what is Britain's most unwanted wedding gift or who the British public would most like to have round for dinner. Guests Claudia Winkelman, Kate Humble, David O'Doherty and Nick Helm join host Jimmy Carr.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: Revealing results of opinion polls carried out around the country.

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway?
programme.desc: The team are joined by guest comic Heather Anne Campbell and professional skateboarder Tony Hawk, as they play games including Weird Newscasters, Song Styles, Themed Restaurant, Scenes from a Hat, and Helping Hands.

programme.title: Al Murray: The Pub Landlord Beautiful British Tour -- Live at the O2
programme.desc: One of the most popular live stand-up comedians of recent years, Al Murray returns as the hilarious, irreverent and hugely entertaining Pub Landlord.

programme.title: Kevin Bridges: A Whole Different Story
programme.desc: Kevin Bridges on stage during his sold-out 2015 tour of the UK. Kevin talks about his journey from Glasgow to the middle classes, having money for the very first time, and even politics and the Scottish referendum.

programme.title: Kerry Godliman: Bandwidth
programme.desc: Kerry Godliman blends rage and chaotic hilarity. Updates on wellness, influencers, robots, and fashion? This is the life of a middle-aged woman who has not got the bandwidth for any of this.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Beverly runs into her enemy, Betsy Rubenstone. Meanwhile, Murray and Adam try to rent a video but are forced to pay a late fee.

programme.desc: Bride Lauren and music-mad Perry look back on their gig-themed wedding at a popular London music venue. Along with with best man Michael and bridesmaid Lee, they discuss what happens when a mod rocker puts his shy bride centre stage.

programme.desc: From jet pack hockey to cake launching, the teams build visual representations of wacky new sports for the Olympics. Who'll win Lego gold?

programme.title: Rude(ish) Tube
programme.desc: A short, cheeky clip from Rude Tube's naughty little sister that features a variety of cats doing strange things, some soldiers stuck in the sand and a dog balancing on a rope.

programme.desc: Breakfast with Borneo's orangutans is a magical way to start the day, but our racers shouldn't hang around too long... Who'll secure a spot in the grand finale?

programme.title: Married at First Sight USA
programme.desc: The honeymoons are coming to an end, and normal life is looming. And that's really quite daunting for some of the less lovey-dovey couples.

programme.title: Married at First Sight USA
programme.desc: The newly-weds are brought back to earth with a bump when they move in together and start facing the challenges - and arguments - of regular married life.

programme.title: Married at First Sight USA
programme.desc: After an explosive meeting with an expert, one couple are left to pick up the pieces of their relationship. But is another pair's marriage already beyond fixing?

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon visits Italian bistro Charlie's in La Verne, Southern California, which was bought in 2010 by Tatiana Leiva, her mother and her sister. Tatiana didn't actually put any money into the purchase herself, but her hard-working mother and sister did because they felt they were helping to fulfil Tatiana's dream. They even put up their houses as collateral! Unfortunately, soon Charlie's was in trouble.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay travels to Baltimore to try to turn around a restaurant that is suffering because its owner, Denise Whiting, has managed to alienate the surrounding community. When Denise opened Café Hon in 1992 it was a roaring success, but as a result of her unwavering business ambition the local community decided to boycott the restaurant, leaving Denise frustrated by falling sales.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Homer learns he needs a heart bypass operation, he searches for the cheapest doctor.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Springfield decides to install a monorail system, Homer gets the job as conductor, but has no idea what he's doing, although he does get to wear a cape. When the monorail opens, all hell breaks loose and it's down to Homer and Bart to save the passengers.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After Homer gets sick from a giant sandwich he's been eating for months, Aunt Selma steps in to take the kids to a new alcohol-based theme park, with near-disastrous consequences.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Homer is late picking up Bart from a school football game, Bart protests by hooking himself up with Tom, a surrogate dad from an agency. Stung by being jilted by his own son, Homer gets into a monster brawl with Tom that even makes the news.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After Homer fails a breathalyser test driving home from a visit to the Duff brewery, he is forced to quit drinking for a month. Meanwhile, Lisa buys a hamster for her school science project, in order to prove that Bart is dumber than a rodent.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge gives the kids an unforgettable history lesson with three tales about Henry VIII, US explorers Lewis and Clark, and the bitter rivalry between Mozart and Salieri.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Milhouse and his mother relocate to Capital City, Bart strikes up a friendship with Lisa, and Homer makes money by pretending to be a transient.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: A musical comes to life in Springfield as Marge stages a revival of her beloved high school show -- but her wonderful memories are threatened by the return of a rival from the past.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Grampa falls victim to a phone scammer, the Simpsons set out on a mission of vengeance to get his money back.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bong Joon-ho's "Parasite," Bambi's revenge and much, much more are featured in the annual Halloween special.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Evergreen Terrace is overrun with traffic and Moe has a fateful choice to make.

programme.title: Dog
programme.desc: With a dog named Lulu by his side, Army Ranger Briggs races down the Pacific Coast to make it to a soldier's funeral on time. Along the way, Briggs and Lulu drive each other completely crazy, break a handful of laws and narrowly evade death.

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: The best moments of Series 7, as a rolling cast of Britain's favourite famous faces tuned in for the best TV on the box.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britain's sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the week's biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: Gogglebox

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Tattooed yoga lover Jack, vibrant 70-year-old fashion stylist Christine and Stetson-wearing Simon from Dallas are among the diners looking for love.

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Dina, who's 39, is keen to settle down and start a family. Her date, 41-year-old Stephen, is a born adventurer who has climbed the Matterhorn and swum oceans; will he run a mile when Dina shares her intentions with him?

programme.title: Gogglebox

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In and around Nottingham, young lovebirds Gabz and Pete kick off the competition by cooking the food they fell in love eating together. They go up against Sushma and Gulab and country-dwelling partners John and Shaun.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In East Sussex, Freddy and Anita serve up a traditional Ghanaian menu which proves to be quite controversial, with cow's feet and tripe too much for Peter and Lisa to swallow.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Derby nurse David hopes to impress his brainy prospective bride Lucy by arranging a wedding based on her favourite book, `Pride and Prejudice', despite knowing nothing about the classic Jane Austen tale. Lucy dreams of something a little more traditional, so can this groom win her over by taking on her favourite love story?

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.sub-title: The One with the Fisherman's Wedding
programme.desc: Harry, 24, and his 28-year-old fiancée Mel live in a beautiful seaside village called Manorbier on the Pembrokeshire coast in south Wales. So, does Harry put his love of the sea before the love of his life? With a bride that finds it hard to trust him, will he get led astray? Can this fisherman really give this land-loving girl the wedding of her dreams?

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Luke, 22, and Alex, 25, met ten months ago when working on Ibiza. Despite not being together for very long, they knew they are made for each other. So after just six months, Luke decided to propose. Money is tight, but this hasn't stopped Alex dreaming of a traditional wedding, surrounded by all her friends and family. However, Luke has got very different ideas and tries to pull off the best Ibiza wedding ever.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.sub-title: The One with the Big Kid's Wedding
programme.desc: Kara, 25, is moving out of her house in Reading and leaving the keys in the tattooed hands of her 22-year-old fiancé, Alex. Kara is petrified about leaving him home alone in her house, especially as he has a reputation of partying hard and being so bad with money that she even has to babysit his bankcard. Alex is a care worker and has never organised anything this big before.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Ex-footballer Steven, 26, tries so hard to pull off the wedding of his dreams, but when he's confronted with his furious bride-to-be Kaleigh on their wedding day, does he finally realise he's made a huge mistake? Steven decides to have their wedding at Thorpe Park, but his idea of putting Kaleigh on the Stealth ride before they've even tied the knot comes crashing down when she refuses to do it.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Joe DiDomizio is the president and CEO of Hudson Group, a travel retailer that operates newsstands, bookshops, cafés and speciality stores in 70 airports and other transportation terminals throughout North America.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Utah Jazz CEO Greg Miller's team spirit is tested when he is asked to be a human basketball hoop while he is undercover.

programme.title: Ramsay's Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Gordon visits Meson de Mesilla, a Tuscan-style hotel in Las Cruces, New Mexico.

programme.title: Ramsay's Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Gordon checks in to the Monticello Hotel in Longview, Washington, which is owned by Phillip Lovingfoss. He's a wealthy individual with a fleet of classic cars, an array of diamond rings and an apparent drinking problem.

programme.title: Ramsay's Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Gordon visits Applegate, Oregon, to try to rescue the Applegate River Lodge, which is around a million dollars in debt. Gordon questions Applegate owner Richard Davis' interest in smoking cannabis.

programme.title: Ramsay's Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Gordon visits Starkville, Mississippi, to try to rescue the failing Hotel Chester. Despite having over 40 years' experience in the hotel business, owner David Mollendor is at a loss about how to make his dream of owning his own hotel work.

programme.title: Ramsay's Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Gordon visits West Dover in Vermont to try to save a dog-friendly hotel that's desperately in need of help. Sandy MacDougall, the owner, runs the Four Seasons Inn.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts as Lee Mack and Joe Wilkinson take on Jon Richardson and Jessica Knappett. Comedian Nigel Ng joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts with Countdown regulars Rachel Riley and Susie Dent as Jack Dee and Tom Allen take on Jon Richardson and Judi Love. Dr John Cooper Clarke is in Dictionary Corner.

programme.title: Russell Howard & Mum: USA Road Trip
programme.desc: Russell and his mum venture south to the Hip Hop Capital of the USA.

programme.title: Russell Howard & Mum: USA Road Trip
programme.desc: Russell and his mum head to one of the USA's most haunted cities: Savannah, Georgia.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr and team captains Jon Richardson and Sean Lock are joined by celebrity guests Steve Jones and David O'Doherty.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the topical comedy panel show, with team captains Jon Richardson and Sean Lock. The teams are joined by a host of celebrity guests.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts as Lee Mack and Joe Wilkinson take on Jon Richardson and Jessica Knappett. Comedian Nigel Ng joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.sub-title: The One at a Naval College
programme.desc: Essex boy Andrew, 27, is a groom torn between two worlds. On one side is his former boss in the Royal Navy and bride-to-be Amanda, who wants Andrew to man up and take charge of the wedding. Although Amanda left the navy three years ago, she still gives the orders in their relationship, but in his desperation to please her, will he make the wrong decision?

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: The pressure is on for 30-year-old sci-fi fanatic Charles as he tries to impress his more classy bride-to-be, 26-year-old Lauren. Originally both from Yorkshire, they met five years ago when they worked behind a bar in Newcastle and have been inseparable ever since. Lauren is an art teacher with very sophisticated tastes and prefers the finer things in life, whereas Charles is an only child who has never grown up.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.sub-title: The One with the Worst Hen Do Ever
programme.desc: Tomboy Anna, 31, tries to plan the perfect day for fiancee Sian, 24. The couple have been inseparable since they met in a bar, and four months later Anna knew Sian was `the one' and promptly asked the question.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Three previously featured bosses ask employees to go under cover for a second look inside their organisations.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Jeff Platt, president and CEO of entertainment and fitness business Sky Zone, a chain of indoor trampoline parks, goes undercover in his own company posing as a contestant on a reality TV competition who is trying to win an action-adventure holiday.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Edinburgh, young lovebirds Andy and Pim are hoping to impress their guests with a Scottish and Thai-themed menu, with a bit of American thrown in for good measure.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Kicking off the competition in Glasgow are Jean and hubby Ron who never uses one word if he can use fifty. Will their taste of Scotland menu impress more than Ron's longwinded stories?

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The competitive dining show heads to the Sussex coast, where childhood sweethearts Jemma and James kick off with a menu based on their unlikely romance.

programme.title: Ramsay's Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay checks into Murphy's Historic Hotel near Sacramento in northern California, where owners Brian, Kevin and Joel are often too distracted by the party going on in the rowdy bar to look after their hotel.

programme.title: Ramsay's Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Gordon checks in to Connecticut's oldest inn, the Curtis House in Woodbury. This historic inn has been owned by the Hardisty family for generations, but the business has started to fall apart and their legacy is on the brink of being lost.

programme.title: Ramsay's Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Gordon checks in to the Historic Calumet Inn, in Pipestone, Minnesota. Vanda and Rina Smrkovski were given the inn as a gift by their father.

programme.title: Ramsay's Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Gordon visits a lodge in Idaho that was built by the owner's own hands before the entrepreneur lost his passion due to a family tragedy.

programme.title: Ramsay's Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Gordon heads to Southbridge, Massachusetts, and checks into the Vienna Inn, an Austrian-themed hotel and restaurant. Owners Jonathan and Lisa have seen their cluttered inn acquire a reputation among the locals, and business isn't what it used to be.

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped
programme.desc: In an episode of unusual events, Joanna Page and Amy Gledhill put pineapples and balls of ice in unexpected places, while Kumail Nanjiani reveals his missing body part.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy game show mash-up. Roisin Conaty and Kiri Pritchard-McLean take on Jon Richardson and Rob Beckett. With Huge Davies, Susie Dent and Rachel Riley.

programme.title: Russell Howard & Mum: USA Road Trip
programme.desc: Russell and his mum are in South Carolina, where they have joined an All-American Demolition Derby.

programme.title: Russell Howard & Mum: USA Road Trip
programme.desc: Russell and his mum's American Adventure leads them to the Big Apple, where they do things doggy style; getting up close and personal with New York's most pampered pooches.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the topical comedy panel show, with team captains Jon Richardson and Sean Lock. The teams are joined by guests Dr Christian Jessen, Lethal Bizzle, Joe Wilkinson and Aisling Bea for this edition.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the topical comedy panel show, with team captains Jon Richardson and Sean Lock. The teams are joined by celebrity guests Adam Hills, Phil Spencer and Henning Wehn.

programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy game show mash-up. Roisin Conaty and Kiri Pritchard-McLean take on Jon Richardson and Rob Beckett. With Huge Davies, Susie Dent and Rachel Riley.

programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the topical comedy panel show, with team captains Jon Richardson and Sean Lock. The teams are joined by guests Dr Christian Jessen, Lethal Bizzle, Joe Wilkinson and Aisling Bea for this edition.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the topical comedy panel show, with team captains Jon Richardson and Sean Lock. The teams are joined by celebrity guests Adam Hills, Phil Spencer and Henning Wehn.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: When Pops goes to the shop with Murray for a little change of pace, he criticizes how Murray does business. Erica and Barry give Adam advice when Dana Caldwell comes over to study.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Beverly runs into her enemy, Betsy Rubenstone. Meanwhile, Murray and Adam try to rent a video but are forced to pay a late fee.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride: Revisited
programme.desc: Bride Lauren and music-mad Perry look back on their gig-themed wedding at a popular London music venue. Along with with best man Michael and bridesmaid Lee, they discuss what happens when a mod rocker puts his shy bride centre stage.

programme.title: LEGO Masters: Australia vs The World
programme.desc: From jet pack hockey to cake launching, the teams build visual representations of wacky new sports for the Olympics. Who'll win Lego gold?

programme.title: Rude(ish) Tube
programme.desc: A short, cheeky clip from Rude Tube's naughty little sister that features a variety of cats doing strange things, some soldiers stuck in the sand and a dog balancing on a rope.

programme.title: The Amazing Race Australia: Celebrity Edition
programme.desc: Breakfast with Borneo's orangutans is a magical way to start the day, but our racers shouldn't hang around too long... Who'll secure a spot in the grand finale?

programme.title: Married at First Sight USA
programme.desc: The honeymoons are coming to an end, and normal life is looming. And that's really quite daunting for some of the less lovey-dovey couples.

programme.title: Married at First Sight USA
programme.desc: The newly-weds are brought back to earth with a bump when they move in together and start facing the challenges - and arguments - of regular married life.

programme.title: Married at First Sight USA
programme.desc: After an explosive meeting with an expert, one couple are left to pick up the pieces of their relationship. But is another pair's marriage already beyond fixing?

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon visits Italian bistro Charlie's in La Verne, Southern California, which was bought in 2010 by Tatiana Leiva, her mother and her sister. Tatiana didn't actually put any money into the purchase herself, but her hard-working mother and sister did because they felt they were helping to fulfil Tatiana's dream. They even put up their houses as collateral! Unfortunately, soon Charlie's was in trouble.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay travels to Baltimore to try to turn around a restaurant that is suffering because its owner, Denise Whiting, has managed to alienate the surrounding community. When Denise opened Café Hon in 1992 it was a roaring success, but as a result of her unwavering business ambition the local community decided to boycott the restaurant, leaving Denise frustrated by falling sales.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Homer learns he needs a heart bypass operation, he searches for the cheapest doctor.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Springfield decides to install a monorail system, Homer gets the job as conductor, but has no idea what he's doing, although he does get to wear a cape. When the monorail opens, all hell breaks loose and it's down to Homer and Bart to save the passengers.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After Homer gets sick from a giant sandwich he's been eating for months, Aunt Selma steps in to take the kids to a new alcohol-based theme park, with near-disastrous consequences.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Homer is late picking up Bart from a school football game, Bart protests by hooking himself up with Tom, a surrogate dad from an agency. Stung by being jilted by his own son, Homer gets into a monster brawl with Tom that even makes the news.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After Homer fails a breathalyser test driving home from a visit to the Duff brewery, he is forced to quit drinking for a month. Meanwhile, Lisa buys a hamster for her school science project, in order to prove that Bart is dumber than a rodent.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge gives the kids an unforgettable history lesson with three tales about Henry VIII, US explorers Lewis and Clark, and the bitter rivalry between Mozart and Salieri.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Milhouse and his mother relocate to Capital City, Bart strikes up a friendship with Lisa, and Homer makes money by pretending to be a transient.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: A musical comes to life in Springfield as Marge stages a revival of her beloved high school show -- but her wonderful memories are threatened by the return of a rival from the past.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Grampa falls victim to a phone scammer, the Simpsons set out on a mission of vengeance to get his money back.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bong Joon-ho's "Parasite," Bambi's revenge and much, much more are featured in the annual Halloween special.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Evergreen Terrace is overrun with traffic and Moe has a fateful choice to make.

programme.title: Dog
programme.desc: With a dog named Lulu by his side, Army Ranger Briggs races down the Pacific Coast to make it to a soldier's funeral on time. Along the way, Briggs and Lulu drive each other completely crazy, break a handful of laws and narrowly evade death.

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: The best moments of Series 7, as a rolling cast of Britain's favourite famous faces tuned in for the best TV on the box.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britain's sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the week's biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: Gogglebox

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Tattooed yoga lover Jack, vibrant 70-year-old fashion stylist Christine and Stetson-wearing Simon from Dallas are among the diners looking for love.

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Dina, who's 39, is keen to settle down and start a family. Her date, 41-year-old Stephen, is a born adventurer who has climbed the Matterhorn and swum oceans; will he run a mile when Dina shares her intentions with him?
